column3: A scientific or technical examination of a physical thing in which one or more properties are investigated to gain knowledge. Covers the full range of scientific examination from visual inspection through to instrumental analysis. May produce simple recorded findings, complex data outputs, or images. S27 is the superclass of S21: Measurement (E16); quantitative measurement events should use the dedicated Measurement model.

This model records a scientific or technical examination event. It captures identification, description, event typing, responsible actors, time and place, the entity observed, the property type examined, methods and protocols followed, equipment employed, and the outputs produced. Outputs may include images (EX_Digital_Image), digital data objects such as spectra or datasets (D1: Digital Object), and documentary records (E73: Observation Record). Interpretation of results and knowledge creation derived from outputs are out of scope here.

A curated physical artefact that contains or carries the sampled matter (S13). Serves as the object-management layer: it holds identifiers, condition records, custody, storage, and images. P46 is used as a pragmatic contains/carries proxy for collection management purposes.

The removed matter treated as an amount of matter (CRMsci S13) whose identity is grounded in material continuity and representativity. Serves as the scientific layer: it is the subject of measurement, observation, modification, and splitting events.
The actor (individual or organisation) responsible for carrying out or authorising the observation.

column1: //links E39: Institution or Person --> person, organisation [confirmed]

column3: The place where the observation occurred (e.g., laboratory, examination room, imaging suite).
Documents that describe or evidence the observation (e.g., examination reports, condition reports, analytical notes).
A defined method or protocol followed during the observation (e.g., a standard examination procedure or analytical protocol).
Specific instruments or equipment used to carry out the observation (e.g., microscope, spectrometer, camera rig, SEM).

High-level technique categories (e.g., spectroscopy, imaging, microscopy, visual examination, diffraction).
The type of property being investigated (e.g., colour, fluorescence response, surface morphology, elemental composition, layer structure, molecular composition).
The timespan during which the observation occurred.
Type/category of instrument or equipment (e.g., optical microscope, SEM, XRF spectrometer, FTIR spectrometer, multispectral camera, X-ray tube).

The type of data output produced (e.g., XRF spectrum, Raman spectrum, EDS map, FTIR spectrum, XRD pattern, numerical dataset).
A textual or structured documentary record produced by the observation event, such as examination notes, an analytical summary, or a condition observation report.
